Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We list the activities: RO, CA, RI, PI, QC, PO, GSL, D, IC, RP. We analyze the process flow: The process starts with RO Then CA must happen If items are available then RI Then PI Then QC Then PO Then GSL Then D Additionally after the order is dispatched we have IC and RP

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Lets assume the standard flow is RO CA RI PI QC PO GSL D then possibly in any order IC and RP We assume that for every order eventually payment is recorded Lets assume each activity occurs exactly once in a standard case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Equivalence Which activities must occur the same number of times We are setting each to 1 so they are all equivalent However we can leave the equivalence set empty

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Always Before pairs such that if the first activity occurs then the second must have occurred before it For CA if CA occurs then RO must have occurred before So CA RO For RI RI RO and RI CA and so on for all subsequent activities all previous ones

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Always After If the first activity occurs then the second must occur after it For RO if RO occurs then every other activity must occur after For CA then RI PI QC PO GSL D IC RP and so on

Name: Deductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Never Together Pairs of activities that cannot occur in the same case In our process all activities occur in the same case So we dont have any never together constraints We leave this set empty

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Activity Occurrences We assume each activity occurs exactly once So activ_freq RO 1 CA 1 RI 1 PI 1 QC 1 PO 1 GSL 1 D 1 IC 1 RP 1

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Directly-Follows Constraints pairs such that if the first occurs then the second must immediately follow it RO immediately followed by CA CA by RI RI by PI PI by QC QC by PO PO by GSL GSL by D

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - NE
Text: activities RO CA RI PI QC PO GSL D IC RP for i act_i in enumerateactivities for j act_j if i j always_beforeadd act_i act_j if i j always_afteradd act_i act_j This generates all transitive pairs assuming total order IC before RP

Name: Validation - IND
Text: note that the process might have some flexibility IC and RP can happen in any order after D We do not have a direct follow constraint for D and the last two activities
